blocks.text: T-CFG treats provider-native config overlay as default-deny. Primary instruction file materialization is the deterministic default surface owned by C6 Workspace.

items: User-global native config overlay is not allowed by default.
C6 never reads or copies global provider config homes.
Provider-native hook settings, task-scoped config home, wrapper manifest, and MCP config injection are active only when the policy bundle explicitly allows that surface.
Codex does not create task-scoped `CODEX_HOME`; Codex app-server may use the existing user Codex auth store.
Codex launch shape is fixed by C4 as `codex --sandbox danger-full-access app-server --listen stdio://`.
Workdir is provider cwd and evidence root, not filesystem sandbox boundary.
Team id, OpenAPI key, and workspace task-location are not Codex auth or sandbox bridge inputs.
Runtime `.codex` state under workdir is provider runtime state, not C6 auth copy/symlink/SaaS credential snapshot.
Dirty workdir policy/config changes use T-POLICY / T-CONFIG runtime upgrade flow, not automatic in-place reinjection.

blocks.text: Source evidence is anchored by `EvaluateNativeConfigHook`, `EvaluateNativeConfigFile`, supervisor policy conversion, disabled Codex config home defaults, and `NativeConfigVersion` policy-bound hashing.
